commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: refactor(ai-chat): extract methods and reduce complexity in AiChatStore
commits.body:
- Inline #normalizeApiMessages into #toApiMessages (remove indirection)
- Extract #buildApiPayload from sendMessage
- Extract #handleStreamError from inline catch block
- Inline temporary variables into .push() calls
- Rename #isStreamTimeoutError to #isTimeoutError

commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: fix(ai-chat): prevent localStorage quota overflow when sending images
commits.body:
Pasting a base64 image (~2–5 MB) into the chat and sending caused
PersistedState to exceed the ~5 MB localStorage quota. The push was
silently reverted, resulting in an empty messages array sent to the
backend ("At least one message is required.").

Fix: strip base64 image data via a custom PersistedState serializer,
pre-build the API payload before touching persisted state, and cache
full image content in a SvelteMap so thumbnails remain visible during
the session.

commits.author: Kevin Geary
commits.subject: feat(canvas): add zoom and scalable canvas width (#262)
commits.body:
Add Option+Scroll zoom (0.25x–1x), editable width/scale inputs in a
canvas size badge, and auto-scaling when the requested width exceeds the
viewport. Counter-scale overlays so element badges remain readable.
Container query generation should be unaffected while media query
generation responds accordingly with canvas scaling.

---------

ETC-556.title: Void element duplicated on the frontend
ETC-556.description:
Turns out before [ETC-550](https://linear.app/digital-gravy/issue/ETC-550/alpine-js-specifc-attribtues-stripped-on-frontend) the faulty DomParser (the PHP native one) we previously used fixed some of the invalid HTML, like turning

into a single
. We did not have tests in place for void element handling so this caused a regression that slipped through the cracks. Solution: Add proper void element handling and tests for it.

ETC-555.title: Add Repeater Prop input in GB
ETC-555.description:
* The user can see the repeater group prop when using the component
* The user can press an "Add" button to add a nested object to the repeater
* The user can delete nested object groups
* The user can add as many nested object as he wants
* The nested object inputs follow the definition of the props (nested text prop renders as text prop)
* The user can populate that data
* The prop references in the component show the data that was input on instance level
* The user can collapse the repeater and the nested groups
